_source: Legacy FUMBBL corpus historically labelled BB2025, measured 2026-06-08 (D61); exact embedded-edition provenance was not retained
_audit_status: LEGACY MIXED-SEMANTICS REFERENCE: do not use this file as a reward target. The 2026-07-09 audit found incompatible FUMBBL-roll versus engine-decision counters, an unreproducible/mixed snapshot, synthetic dispersion, and ignored covariance. Retained for historical dashboard compatibility only.
_note: Per-game rates from full human games (kickoff to end). Compare ONLY against full-game-from-kickoff agent eval (tools/eval_game_stats.sh, demo_reset_pct 0) — training dashboards use curriculum starts and are NOT comparable. Human is a PLAUSIBLY-OPTIMAL reference, not a ceiling: a superhuman policy may legitimately diverge on context metrics (fewer/better blocks, more ball security, fewer GFIs).
dodge_success_per_game: ~unknown (dodgeRoll has no success flag in corpus; attempts only)
_handoff_note: measured 2026-06-10 via turnDataSetHandOverUsed=true over 500-replay sample (663 uses); handoff has no thrower roll so the D61 roll-type count missed it
_possession_note: RETIRED D90 value. It counted gameSetHomePlaying toggles from setup, kickoff, defensive windows, and cleanup rather than genuine regular team turns, and it mixed rules editions.
_possession_bb2025_note: Exact embedded rulesVersion=BB2025 census on 2026-07-09 using tools/human_possession.py. A genuine regular turn opens on turnDataSetTurnStarted=true and closes on turnEnd; touchdown-ending turns count as held, and half/match cleanup is evaluated from the pre-cleanup state. Descriptive diagnostic only, not an optimization target.
_decision_closer_bb2025_note: Exact 11,580-game BB2025 audit using initial non-rerolled FUMBBL test reports and one record per resolved block. These are closer to engine decision counters than the legacy all-roll census but still count tests reached rather than every intended action. Diagnostic only.
